subject: UX & Research
title: Color Contrast and Readability
description: A quick, practical guide to Color Contrast and Readability.

hook.text: Light gray on white looks elegant. For 217 million people with visual impairments, it's unreadable.

buildup.text: WCAG requires 4.5:1 contrast for normal text and 3:1 for large text. Simple math, huge impact.

discovery.text: The WebAIM contrast checker takes 2 seconds. Paste two hex colors and get an instant pass or fail.

twist.text: Many trendy palettes fail contrast. Pastel text on light backgrounds looks nice but excludes millions.

climax.text: Fix contrast early. Redesigning entire color systems later is painful. Test colors during the design phase.

punchline.text: 4.5:1 ratio, 2-second test. Contrast is the easiest accessibility win.

quiz.question: What is the minimum WCAG contrast ratio for normal text?
options: 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text
